fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Mexico Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Mexico - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity new mexico, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to new mexico, respiratory therapist compact new mexico, new mexico respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: New Mexico Board of Respiratory Care Practitioners
board.parent_dept: New Mexico Regulation and Licensing Department
board.mailing_address:
New Mexico Medical Board
2055 S. Pacheco St., Bldg. 4
Santa Fe, NM 87505

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 24 contact hours of continuing education every two years

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in New Mexico.

steps.title: Create Account and Start Online Application
steps.description: Visit the New Mexico Medical Board Online Application and Renewal Portal (app.nmmb.nm.gov), click 'Register' to create a new user account, and then proceed to complete the Respiratory Care Practitioner application.

steps.title: Submit Required Documents
steps.description: Upload or arrange for official transcripts from an approved Respiratory Care program and official NBRC score reports (verifying RRT credentialing) to be sent directly to the NMMB. A copy of your NBRC certificate should also be submitted.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Undergo a fingerprint-based criminal background check. Follow the instructions provided by the NMMB for using approved LiveScan vendors or submitting fingerprint cards via IdentoGO.

steps.title: Request License Verifications
steps.description: If you hold or have held an RCP license in any other state or jurisdiction, arrange for official verification of licensure to be sent directly from each issuing board to the New Mexico Medical Board.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $150.00 through the New Mexico Medical Board Online Application and Renewal Portal.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application through your account on the New Mexico Medical Board Online Application and Renewal Portal and promptly respond to any requests for additional information.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a New Mexico respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does New Mexico have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in New Mexico. To work in New Mexico, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to New Mexico?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Mexico while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in New Mexico
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Therapist license in New Mexico, applicants must use the New Mexico Medical Board Online Application and Renewal Portal to submit their application and supporting documents.
